characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The main protagonist. He is subsequently shocked and overwhelmed by his first transport to Chaotic. Tom is a bold risk taker and will help anyone who asks, even the inhabitants of Perim. Tom is honest, trustworthy and cares deeply for his friends. Audience Surrogate: For most of season 1, he serves as this, since Tom used to be a newbie in Chaotic. Badass Normal: He's a pretty athletic teenager, with bravery and wits apparent as well. As for specific examples:His ability to use a scanner has helped him reach creatures to warn them of danger, such as "Eye of the Maelstrom".His tenacity allowed him to trick both Chaor and Lord Van Bloot into taking the wrong Colestone from him, giving him the time needed to get the real one (containing all of Maxxor's captured strength) to Maxxor. His ability to use a scanner has helped him reach creatures to warn them of danger, such as "Eye of the Maelstrom". His tenacity allowed him to trick both Chaor and Lord Van Bloot into taking the wrong Colestone from him, giving him the time needed to get the real one (containing all of Maxxor's captured strength) to Maxxor.

characters.name: Players

characters.name: CodeMasters
characters.description: Adults Are Useless: Downplayed. However, they aren't always so reliable as authority figures. In fact, aside from letting creeps like Klay and Krystella continue to hang around in Chaotic, their method of dealing with the M'arrillian invasion had noticeable faults. Asskicking Leads to Leadership: According tosupplemental material, theCodeMasterswere ordinary players who were granted their titles after proving their dominance in the game. The Faceless: AllCodeMasterswear face-concealing helmets as part of their outfit. However, Crellan first meets Tom in casual dress that reveals his face (assuming that's what he actually looks like). Fun with Acronyms: Their names spell out the initialism "CHAOTIC" (or "COTHICA"). The Ghost: Amzen, Oron, Tirasis, and Chirrul are mentioned throughout the series, but are never shown onscreen.

characters.name: Overworld Tribe
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Population: Humanoid Overworlders usually have green or blue skin. Beast Man: A large number of Overworlders are humanoid animals. Grey-and-Gray Morality: Their relationship with others in general. While Tom initially treated the Overworld Tribe as the most heroic in Perim, he soon learns that many Overworlders can be incredibly hostile and nasty. The ruler of the Overworld tribe, known in the card game as an Overworld Hero. He is known for his courage. He is the archenemy of Chaor. He is friends with Tom due to the particular way in which the human idolizes him, going so far as to trust him with some missions.In the game, Maxxor is an Overworld creature with Fire and Earth and 2 Mugic abilities that can spend 1 Mugic counter to heal an ally by 10. His second form, Maxxor, Protector of Perim, retains Fire and Earth, has 0 Mugic ability, and gains a stacking damage bonus to his own attacks whenever he takes attack damage. Amazing Technicolor Population: His appearance is overall human-like, save for his green skin.

characters.name: Underworld Tribe
characters.description: Our Monsters Are Different: Very different. Some Underworlders look like classical demons or beings from other mythologies, and some aren't even humanoid. Not Always Evil: Despite serving as antagonists more often than the other tribes, some Underworlders are friendly and even noble. Sexy Dimorphism: Male Underworlders aren't exactly pretty to look at, while female Underworlders are usuallyCute Monster Girls. The ruler of the Underworld tribe. Chaor is determined to conquer all of Perim, but none more so than the Overworld. He dreams of conquering Kiru City and defeating his enemy, Maxxor, but he only wishes to do so to protect the Underworld. He has an unusual friendship with Kaz. He'll often tell Kaz that friends are a weakness, though he'll still protect the human from danger. His basic card has a Mugic ability of 3, the Fire element, and the ability to sacrifice 3 Mugic Counters to dispel any Overworld Mugic or ability that targets him. Chaor the Fierce has a Mugic ability of 0, the Fire element and Fire 10.

characters.name: Mipedian Tribe
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: Mipedians despise Danians far more than the other tribes. This is visibly toned down after the M'arrillian Invasion, though. Lizard Folk: Mipedians are, as a whole, humanoid lizards of different sizes and colors. Minor Major Character: Crown Prince Iflar, the actual leader of the tribe, who only appears briefly in one episode. Non-Mammalian Hair: Some Mipedians are just as reptilian as the rest of their tribe, but they have hair. Mudeenu is a Mipedian Royal and Captain of the Guard. His cousin is Crown Prince Iflar. In game Mudeenu has two versions with different abilities:

characters.name: Danian Tribe
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: They loathe Mipedians far more than any other tribe. This is visibly toned down after the M'arrillian Invasion, though. Insectoid Aliens: They are sentient ants, only they're native of Perim like the rest of the tribes. A lazy Danian Mandiblor that assists Sarah in navigating the inner workings of Mount Pillar.In the game Wamma has three versions with different abilities: His basic version has a Mugic ability of 0, Fire elemental type and the Hive ability to gain 5 energy for each Mandiblor you control. Wamma, Hive Ordinance also has a Mugic Ability of 0 and the Fire elemental type. He gains a Mugic Counter every time a Mandiblor comes into play from the discard pile and makes it so infected creatures don't reveal their Battlegear at the beginning of combat.

characters.name: M'arrillian Tribe
characters.description: The long-forgotten fifth Tribe of Perim, sealed behind the Doors of the Deepmines long ago. Brainwashed and Crazy: They can brainwash other creatures and even humans to do their bidding. CCG Importance Dissonance: The show repeatedly refers to the tribe as an immense force. The thing is, though, the card game didn't do a very good job of portraying that, in no small part from their comparative lack of synergy with one another, as well as most Chieftains being gimmicky on their own and lacking Mugic counters. Conflict Killer: Their threat becomes so prevalent that the other four tribes make a truce to stop them. Once the M'arrillian invasion fails, the four tribes dissolve their alliance, though with far less hostility than before. Dark Is Not Evil: Like with the other Tribes, not all M'arrillians are hostile. One by the name of Ab'lop even befriended Saand and Peyton, helping the latter out of genuine kindness.

characters.name: Other Creatures
characters.description: A tribeless creature in Prexxor Chasm. He is among the few inhabitants who show compassion towards others. Meaningful Name: His name comes from the Smilodon, duh. Feeling Their Age: Smildon notes this to Tom in their first meeting. Token Heroic Orc: Throughout the TV series, he's the only tribeless creature to show any semblance of friendliness. Took a Level in Kindness: He was initially somewhat brash towards Tom, but learns to trust him over time.
